content: Create a detailed outline for an article explaining the difference between MCP (Modular Capability Providers) and skills, how they complement each other, and the differences in skill requirements and implementation patterns between Claude Code and Codex. The outline should include definitions, functional relationships, technical distinctions, implementation best practices, and practical examples.

### 35. Modular Capability Providers vs. Skills: How AI Agents Get Their Superpowers

**Synopsis**
This article explores the technical and conceptual distinctions between Modular Capability Providers (MCPs) and skills, detailing how each contributes to the extensibility and real-world utility of AI agents. It draws a deep comparison of implementation, requirements, and best practices for using MCPs and skills across two leading agent models—Claude Code and Codex—emphasizing how these components form a synergistic, modular architecture. Readers will leave with clear actionable insights on designing robust agent workflows that combine the strengths of both patterns.

**Lead Intro**
Imagine building an AI that not only understands your company’s documentation but can submit pull requests, trigger deployments, or analyze customer data in real time. The secret lies not just in smarter models, but in how you combine their building blocks: modular capability providers (MCPs) and skills. As platforms rush to offer plug-in extensibility for their next-generation AI agents, the difference between “what an agent can do” and “how it does it” becomes critical. Unpacking this architectural distinction is the key to designing agents that are powerful, composable, and secure—regardless of whether you’re working in Claude Code, OpenAI Codex, or beyond.

**Target Audience**
Engineering Lead

**Key Takeaway**
Designing effective AI agents requires separating the “how” (skills/workflows) from the “with what” (MCP/tools), and tailoring their integration patterns to your deployment platform’s strengths.

**Salable Hook**
Demonstrates expertise in AI agent infrastructure and modular capability architecture—clarifying a subtle but crucial technical distinction that underpins secure, scalable extensibility for enterprise AI.

**Supporting Material**

- [Salable Beta Docs: Modular Capability Providers](https://beta.salable.app/docs/mcp)
- [OpenAI Cookbook: Function Calling and Tool Use](https://cookbook.openai.com/examples/how_to_call_functions_with_gpt)
- [Anthropic Claude Code: Custom Skills and Tool Integrations](https://docs.anthropic.com/claude/docs/custom-skills)
- [In-depth comparison and examples from recent agent infrastructure deployments (see provided memory)](https://beta.salable.app/docs/agent-patterns)

**Estimated Word Count**: 1,900 words

**Content Pillar**: Billing Operations

---

**Sectional Flow**
(Narrative outline for use by the Writer agent)

1. Definitions and Conceptual Foundation
   - Clear, technical definitions of MCPs (what, protocols, role in “real-world” interaction)
   - Clear definition of skills (structured workflows, behavioral logic, SKILL.md format)
2. How MCPs and Skills Work Together
   - The division of labor: skills orchestrate, MCPs act
   - Example narrative: “Deploy with a Skill, call the cloud via MCP”
3. Claude Code vs. Codex: Implementation Patterns and Technical Distinctions
   - Skill format, discovery, and invocation
   - How each platform integrates MCP/tools
   - Portability: open SKILL.md standard and cross-agent skill sharing
   - Case study: code review skill calling linters via MCP in both Claude Code and Codex
4. Implementation Best Practices
   - Progressive loading, limiting tool permissions via MCP
   - Organizing skills for composability and workflow reuse
   - Security and governance: why MCP centralization matters (auth, audit, vendor abstraction)
5. Practical Examples and Reusable Patterns
   - Simple skill: customer feedback analysis that calls a summarizing MCP
   - Complex skill: multi-step deployment pipeline across tools/providers
   - Table: Key contrasts (role, function, implementation, portability, use cases)
6. Final Takeaways
   - When to focus on skills, when to design custom MCPs, how this architecture sets your agents up for secure, scalable extension

---

This structure matches the style and depth of advanced guides in Salable’s content strategy, clarifying a nuanced but foundational concept for technical buyers and architects.

Create an article outline from the research summary on MCPs and skills, their differences, complementarities, and a comparison between Claude Code and Codex skill requirements and implementation patterns. Organize for a technical reader.

### 36. Modular Capability Providers vs. Skills: Architecting Composable AI Agents in Claude Code and Codex

**Synopsis**
This article unpacks the technical distinctions and complementarities between Modular Capability Providers (MCPs) and skills, focusing on their pivotal roles within AI agent architectures such as Claude Code and Codex. It explains how MCPs supply secure real-world tool access and skills encode reusable logic or workflows, and explores how major agent platforms differ in implementation patterns, requirements, and best practices. Readers will learn to design composable, secure, and maintainable AI-powered systems by harnessing the synergy between skills and MCPs.

**Lead Intro**
Deploying AI agents into real production workloads takes more than just clever prompts and model weights. The true leap in capability comes from a modular architecture—where skills encode what the agent knows and how it reasons, and MCPs supply the external superpowers for working with live code, infrastructure, and APIs. For teams building in Claude Code or Codex, understanding how these layers interact is crucial for securely scaling agent autonomy while keeping operations transparent and governable. But the details of how these patterns play out across platforms matter—and determine whether your agents will be powerful, maintainable, or a tangled mess.

**Target Audience**
Engineering Lead

**Key Takeaway**
The most robust AI agents separate reusable reasoning (skills) from external integrations (MCPs), tailoring their wiring and workflows to each platform’s strengths for modularity, security, and maintainability.

**Salable Hook**
Demonstrates expertise in AI agent extensibility standards and modular integration, clarifying best practices that enable secure, scalable, and vendor-neutral agent workflows.

**Supporting Material**

- [Salable Beta Docs: Modular Capability Providers](https://beta.salable.app/docs/mcp)
- [OpenAI Cookbook: Function Calling and Tool Use](https://cookbook.openai.com/examples/how_to_call_functions_with_gpt)
- [Anthropic Claude Docs: Custom Skills & Tool Integrations](https://docs.anthropic.com/claude/docs/custom-skills)
- [Sample Skill & MCP Patterns: Salable Agent Documentation](https://beta.salable.app/docs/agent-patterns)

**Estimated Word Count**: 2,000 words
